key_insight: An adaptive multiscale P-IMPES scheme updates coarse-scale physics only when saturation-driven mobility changes become significant, preserving conservation and accuracy in high-contrast two-phase porous flow.
engineering_relevance: This enables efficient, physically reliable simulation of incompressible immiscible flow in heterogeneous reservoirs while reducing unnecessary multiscale space rebuilds.
method: Couples physics-preserving IMPES with mixed constraint energy-minimizing multiscale finite elements.
Adapts multiscale basis updates using a tolerance on the mobility-weighted permeability change.
Applies local postprocessing to recover fine-grid mass conservation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports analytical guarantees and numerical experiments on multiple high-contrast permeability fields showing improved saturation accuracy with smaller adaptive tolerances and fewer redundant updates.
watch_item: Performance depends on a suitable tolerance and CFL condition; too aggressive updating or violating stability constraints may affect bounds preservation and accuracy.
paper_title: An Adaptive and Physics-Preserving Multiscale Method for Two-Phase Flow Simulations in High-Contrast Heterogeneous Porous Media
